read-only.description: Read files, inspect docs, query the local backlog/audit databases, and run non-mutating checks.

generated-artifact.description: Regenerate docs, database mirrors, reports, and audit-safe artifacts.

code-change.description: Edit application, test, script, docs, and config files in the local repo.

migration.description: Create or modify database migrations or schema-affecting code.

secrets.description: Read, write, rotate, export, or reference secret material.

billing.description: Mutate billing, payment, plan, discount, or invoice state.

production.description: Deploy, push, run production commands, or alter production/provider state.

destructive.description: Delete, reset, force-push, wipe, drop, or irreversibly mutate state.

tierMeanings.asserted: A human or agent claim with no artifact. Never enough to sign off.
tierMeanings.config: The controlling configuration exists (Terraform / env / flag). Not sufficient for a sensitive release class.
tierMeanings.test: An automated check passed (unit / integration / gate).
tierMeanings.live: Observed true in the running or deployed system (probe, response header, measured metric, restore drill).

evidence.releaseGateRule: A backlog item whose feature carries a release-class:<class> label cannot be signed off (status 'verified') on asserted/config/test evidence alone; it requires a backlog event whose evidence_tier is 'live'. Enforced by the release_gate_violations view and npm run backlog:validate.

controls.description: Toggleable policy-as-code guardrail controls. Each control may be enabled or disabled per risk and context, EXCEPT controls pinned alwaysOn:true. Toggling is a governed action: the only supported path is scripts/adg-toggle-control.mjs, which refuses always-on controls, requires reason/risk/rollback, and writes an append-only audit decision event. The deterministic hook and guardrail-check both pin the always-on set in code, so a hand-edit that relaxes an always-on control is ignored at runtime (fail-closed) and rejected by the gate.

destructiveDeny.description: Deny destructive commands (rm -rf, force-push, DROP, TRUNCATE, mkfs, dd) by default. Cannot be disabled.

auditAppendOnly.description: The append-only audit log may not be truncated, overwritten, redirected over, or edited in place. Cannot be disabled.

forbiddenBulkRead.description: Block raw reads of generated bulk artifacts (SQLite, mirrors, dumps); use the context broker. Cannot be disabled.

secretsConfirm.description: Require confirmation before referencing secret material.

productionConfirm.description: Require confirmation before production or deploy commands.

migrationConfirm.description: Require confirmation before migration or schema changes.

billingConfirm.description: Require confirmation before billing or payment state changes.

controlFileGuard.description: Require confirmation before editing governance control files.

assetLint.description: Deterministic quality gate for committed image assets: rejects edge-clipped, blank, or wrong-format exports. Enforced by scripts/asset-lint.mjs (pixel reading by the tools/adg-asset-lint Rust helper); skips green when the helper is not built unless config.onToolMissing is 'block'.
